Title: The gland localized CGP1 controls gland pigmentation and gossypol accumulation in cotton

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; MYB DNA-binding domain; transcription activation domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pigment glands in aerial tissues such as stem and leaf
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cotton defensive secondary metabolites contributing to resistance against pests and pathogens; low seed gossypol is desirable for food/feed use
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation, likely via heterodimerization with GoPGF
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Gossypol and related terpenoid biosynthesis/accumulation in pigment glands
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive; knockout down-regulated most gossypol biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms heterodimer with GoPGF in the nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly correlated with pigment gland density; high in young stem, leaf, petiole, hypocotyl, cotyledon, pedicel and boll shell; very low in roots; promoter-GUS mainly in immature pigment glands
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Almost undetectable in glandless cultivars; expressed in glanded cultivars and positively correlated with gland density
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes gland pigmentation and accumulation of gossypol and related terpenoids, but does not control gland morphogenesis or gland number
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq; VIGS; stable RNAi; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qPCR; promoter-GUS; LC-ESI-MS/MS; HPLC; subcellular localization; yeast transactivation assay; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CGP1a was identified from TFs down-regulated in glandless cotton and its expression strongly correlated with pigment gland density. 2. Silencing or knockout of CGP1a caused a glandless-like visual phenotype due to loss of pigmentation, not loss of gland structures. 3. cgp1 mutants showed strong reductions in gossypol, HGQ and heliocides and down-regulation of most gossypol biosynthetic genes. 4. CGP1a is a functional nuclear MYB activator that interacts with GoPGF in the nucleus, supporting a regulatory role in terpenoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important positive regulator of gossypol/terpenoid accumulation and gland pigmentation, but not essential for gland formation; contributes substantially yet residual gossypol remains in mutants
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modulate gland-contained terpenoids/gossypol; may help maintain aerial defense while reducing seed gossypol for improved cottonseed oil/protein utilization
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing CGP1 lowers defensive terpenoids in aerial tissues, potentially compromising pest/pathogen resistance
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedlings grown in greenhouse at 28°C, 16 h light/8 h dark; metabolite measured in stems and leaves
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for subcellular localization and protein interaction constructs; native CGP1 promoter for GUS; VIGS and CRISPR/Cas9 constructs used for functional tests
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In upland cotton TM-1, CGP1a is the only functional allele because CGP1d contains a 100-bp deletion causing frameshift and loss of transactivation and GoPGF interaction; expression was gland-localized especially in immature glands

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; binds promoters of WRKYs and terpene synthases according to cited prior work; interacts with CGP1a
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms heterodimer with CGP1a in nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed throughout cotton plants; higher than CGP1 in roots according to discussion
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with CGP1a to control terpenoid accumulation; previously established as essential for pigment gland biogenesis and gossypol synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP in this paper; prior knockout/silencing evidence cited in discussion
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GoPGF was identified as a CGP1a-interacting protein in a Y2H screen. 2. Interaction with CGP1a was validated by Y2H, BiFC and Co-IP in the nucleus. 3. The paper supports a model where GoPGF partners with CGP1a to regulate gossypol and related terpenoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential regulator from prior work; in this study mainly supported as CGP1a partner in regulatory control of terpenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking partner with CGP1 for engineering gland traits and gland terpenoid content
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong reduction may abolish glands and gossypol, affecting plant defense
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assays in tobacco leaves; cotton growth in greenhouse
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S or equivalent transient expression promoters in interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because it is directly experimentally tested in Results as a validated CGP1a interactor affecting the same nutrient/secondary metabolite trait
